message:
Add /docs:review command for documentation review

Introduces a new slash command that reviews documentation changes for
completeness and style. The command identifies changes in the docs
repository, starts a preview server, checks completeness using the
docs:writing-documentation skill, and reviews style using the
writing:technical-writing skill.

This command integrates into the documentation workflow as the second
step after /docs:write, improving the overall docs creation experience.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+---
+title: Add /docs:review command for documentation review
+type: feature
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+created: 2025-12-19T06:20:57.742309Z
+---
+
+Introduces the `/docs:review` command to review documentation changes for completeness and style. The command:
+
+- Identifies changes in the `.docs/` directory
+- Starts the local preview server for live verification
+- Checks completeness using the `docs:writing-documentation` skill
+- Reviews writing style using the `writing:technical-writing` skill
+- Summarizes findings and offers to fix identified issues
+
+This integrates seamlessly into the documentation workflow as the second step after `/docs:write`.

@@ -0,0 +1,58 @@
+---
+description: Review documentation changes for completeness and style
+---
+
+# Review documentation
+
+Use the `docs:writing-documentation` and `writing:technical-writing` skills for
+this review.
+
+## 1. Identify changes
+
+Check for uncommitted changes in `.docs/`:
+
+```bash
+cd .docs && git diff --stat
+```
+
+If there are no changes, inform the user and stop.
+
+## 2. Start preview server
+
+Start the local development server:
+
+```bash
+cd .docs && pnpm dev
+```
+
+Tell the user the preview is available at `http://localhost:4321` and that they
+can check the rendered output while you continue with the review.
+
+## 3. Check documentation completeness
+
+According to `docs:writing-documentation`, review the changes for completeness
+across documentation sections. Changes often require updates in multiple
+sections. For example:
+
+- Reference changes may need corresponding guide updates
+- New features often need a guide showing practical usage
+- New concepts in explanations should link to relevant guides
+
+Report any completeness gaps and suggest what additional documentation may be
+needed.
+
+## 4. Review writing style
+
+According to `writing:technical-writing`, check the changed content for style
+issues. Report any issues found with specific suggestions for improvement.
+
+## 5. Summarize review
+
+Provide a summary:
+
+- Files reviewed
+- Completeness issues (if any)
+- Style issues (if any)
+- Recommended next steps
+
+If issues were found, ask if the user wants you to fix them.

@@ -56,5 +56,5 @@ Load the `writing:technical-writing` skill for detailed style guidance.
 ## Workflow
 
 1. Run `/docs:write` to create or update documentation
-2. Preview locally: `cd .docs && pnpm dev`
+2. Run `/docs:review` to check completeness and style
 3. Run `/docs:pr` to create a pull request
